accepted_findings.message_regex: Probable insecure usage of temp file/directory
accepted_findings.reason: False positive. Local developer/CI e2e harness only: fixed paths under /tmp/loga3-shots and /tmp/loga3-smoke-creds.env for screenshots and optional smoke creds. Not shipped in the app binary; no attacker-controlled temp path.

accepted_findings.message_regex: Audit url open for permitted schemes
accepted_findings.reason: False positive. urllib.request.urlopen is hard-coded to http://127.0.0.1:<CDP_PORT>/json/list for Android WebView CDP during local debugging. No remote or file: URL from untrusted input.
False positive. E2E scripts intentionally import subprocess to invoke the local adb binary with argv lists (no shell). Dev/CI tooling only; not app runtime code.

accepted_findings.message_regex: subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input
accepted_findings.reason: False positive. subprocess.run/check_output use fixed adb + literal argument arrays (screencap, shell dumpsys, etc.). No shell=True and no untrusted user argv.
False positive. assert is used as test/smoke postconditions in e2e scripts, not production app code. Optimized bytecode stripping is irrelevant for these developer runners.
False positive. Narrow best-effort cleanup in e2e (ignore missing files/UI nodes). Failures are still raised on the main smoke path; silence is intentional for teardown only.

accepted_findings.message_regex: Standard pseudo-random generators are not suitable for security/cryptographic purposes
accepted_findings.reason: False positive. random.sample only picks two extra calendar months for matrix coverage. Not used for secrets, tokens, or crypto.

Accepted residual risk (transitive). uuid@7 comes via xcode → @expo/config-plugins (EAS/prebuild tooling). App source does not import uuid. GHSA/CVE concerns v3/v5/v6 with caller-supplied buffers; that API is not used by our runtime. Revisit when Expo bumps xcode/uuid.
Accepted residual risk. Build-time iOS project helper; advisory is inherited from uuid. Not present in the Android/iOS app runtime JS bundle path for Holen/sync.
Accepted residual risk. Advisory chain only via xcode→uuid (build/config plugins). No direct runtime exploit path in the mobile app.
Accepted residual risk. Transitive advisory via @expo/config-plugins → xcode → uuid. Dev/prebuild surface only.
Accepted residual risk. Transitive Expo CLI packaging dep; same uuid chain.
Accepted residual risk. Transitive Expo build-cache dep; same uuid chain.
Accepted residual risk. Metro/bundler config dependency; same uuid chain. Not an on-device vulnerability.
Accepted residual risk. Prebuild-time Expo config; same uuid chain.
Accepted residual risk. Expo CLI (dev/build host), not shipped as app logic; advisory is transitive uuid via config-plugins.
Accepted residual risk. Framework package flagged only because it depends on the Expo config/CLI tree that pulls xcode→uuid. No app-code use of vulnerable uuid buffer APIs.
Accepted residual risk. Plugin dependency on @expo/config-plugins; share sheet itself does not invoke uuid v3/v5/v6 buffer writes.
Accepted residual risk. Config-plugin transitive only; splash screen has no uuid buffer API usage.
Accepted residual risk. Flagged only via parent expo advisory chain (uuid). Downgrading to 8.x as npm suggests would be a breaking change unrelated to Google Sign-In security.
Accepted residual risk. Flagged only via peer/parent expo advisory chain (uuid). Widget code does not use uuid.

Accepted residual risk. Transitive uuid@7.0.3 via xcode (Expo config-plugins). Application code never imports uuid; vulnerable pattern requires caller-provided buf/offset to v3/v5/v6. Build-tool only until upstream Expo/xcode bumps uuid. Tracked in docs/security-audit.md follow-ups.
